content.p: Sketch the polar curve defined by r={{f}} and find a difference of integrals which compute the area bound between the inner and outer loop.

outtro.p: Note that multiple expressions for bounding angles are possible. \displaystyle \int_{ {{a2}} }^{ {{b2}} }\left({{fs}} \right) d\theta - \displaystyle \int_{ {{a1}} }^{ {{b1}} }\left({{fs}} \right) d\theta.

content.p: Sketch the polar curves defined by r={{f}} and r={{R}}. Then find an integral which compute the area {{first}} of r={{f}} and {{second}} of r={{R}}.

outtro.p: Note that multiple expressions for bounding angles are possible. \displaystyle \int_{ {{a}} }^{ {{b}} }\left({{fs}} \right) d\theta .
